title: Jhandewalan Temple
tagline: Jhandewalan Temple — spiritual town of Delhi

overview.short: The Jhandewalan Mandir, a Delhi Hindu temple dedicated to Vaishno Devi, was founded in the 19th century by Badri Bhagat, named for a large flag hoisted when a deity was discovered.
overview.description: The Jhandewalan Mandir, a Delhi Hindu temple dedicated to Vaishno Devi, was founded in the 19th century by Badri Bhagat, named for a large flag hoisted when a deity was discovered. Formerly in Ram Bagh, it hosted events like the Pavan Pariksha Mela, which ended after Partition. Now surrounded by urban development, it remains a major religious center, especially during Navratras.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jhandewalan metro station
topPlaces.description: The Jhandewalan metro station is located on the Blue Line of the Delhi Metro.

topPlaces.name: Qadam Sharif (Delhi)
topPlaces.description: The Dargah Qadam Sharif in Paharganj, Delhi consists of a small tomb complex, built in 1375–1376 CE, which also houses a mosque, a madrasa and a shrine ("dargah"), which is surrounded by a massive gated wall.

topPlaces.name: Ajmal Khan Park
topPlaces.description: Ajmal Khan Park is an urban park in the central part of the Karol Bagh of New Delhi, India. Spread over five acres, it was initially opened in 1921, named Hakim Ajmal Khan (1868–1927), noted freedom fighter and Unani medicine physician.

topPlaces.name: Paharganj
topPlaces.description: Paharganj (literally 'hilly neighbourhood') is a neighbourhood of Central Delhi, located just west of the New Delhi Railway Station. Known as Shahganj or King's ganj or market place during Mughal era, it is one of the three administrative subdivisions, of the Central Delhi district, of the National Capital Territory of Delhi, with the other two being, Darya Ganj and Karol Bagh.

topPlaces.name: 2019 Delhi factory fire
topPlaces.description: On 8 December 2019, a fire occurred at a factory building in Anaj Mandi area of Delhi, India. At least 43 people died and more than 56 were injured.

topPlaces.name: Ramakrishna Ashram Marg metro station
topPlaces.description: The Ramakrishna (R.K.) Ashram Marg metro station is located on the Blue Line of the Delhi Metro.

topPlaces.name: 2019 Delhi hotel fire
topPlaces.description: A fire began at a budget hotel in Central Delhi, the Hotel Arpit Palace, in the Karol Bagh area of the city, at around 4:30 am on 12 February 2019, killing at least 17 people. The fire went through all floors of the hotel; people were seen jumping from it.

topPlaces.name: Archeparchy of Faridabad
topPlaces.description: The Archeparchy of Faridabad is an Archeparchy of the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church in Faridabad, a city in Haryana, India. Erected on 6 March 2012 by Pope Benedict XVI it serves around 150,000 Syro-Malabar Catholics in the North India.

items.note: The Jhandewalan metro station is located on the Blue Line of the Delhi Metro.

The Dargah Qadam Sharif in Paharganj, Delhi consists of a small tomb complex, built in 1375–1376 CE, which also houses a mosque, a madrasa and a shrine ("dargah"), which is surrounded by a massive gated wall.

Ajmal Khan Park is an urban park in the central part of the Karol Bagh of New Delhi, India. Spread over five acres, it was initially opened in 1921, named Hakim Ajmal Khan (1868–1927), noted freedom fighter and Unani medicine physician.

Paharganj (literally 'hilly neighbourhood') is a neighbourhood of Central Delhi, located just west of the New Delhi Railway Station. Known as Shahganj or King's ganj or market place during Mughal era, it is one of the three administrative subdivisions, of the Central Delhi district, of the National Capital Territory of Delhi, with the other two being, Darya Ganj and Karol Bagh.
On 8 December 2019, a fire occurred at a factory building in Anaj Mandi area of Delhi, India. At least 43 people died and more than 56 were injured.
The Ramakrishna (R.K.) Ashram Marg metro station is located on the Blue Line of the Delhi Metro.

A fire began at a budget hotel in Central Delhi, the Hotel Arpit Palace, in the Karol Bagh area of the city, at around 4:30 am on 12 February 2019, killing at least 17 people. The fire went through all floors of the hotel; people were seen jumping from it.

The Archeparchy of Faridabad is an Archeparchy of the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church in Faridabad, a city in Haryana, India. Erected on 6 March 2012 by Pope Benedict XVI it serves around 150,000 Syro-Malabar Catholics in the North India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jhandewalan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jhandewalan Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jhandewalan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (14 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jhandewalan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jhandewalan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jhandewalan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Jhandewalan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jhandewalan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jhandewalan metro station, Qadam Sharif (Delhi), Ajmal Khan Park, Paharganj, 2019 Delhi factory fire.
